title: Episode-ordinal subset lint + DA calibration (B0044 premise INVERTED: subsets are legitimate)
source: loop-a-tick #13 (2026-05-26): the DA twice flagged target_regime_episode_ordinals naming specific episodes ([13,15,21] then [1,13,21]) as soft episode-targeting; the lint did not catch it.
why: Loop A tick 13's BULL_STRESSED proposals were BLOCKED partly because the DA read target_regime_episode_ordinals naming a SUBSET ([13,15,21], [1,13,21]) as episode-targeting. Per phase5-regime-methodology SKILL.md:137 a subset is the INTENDED design and only a non-subset should be flagged; the DA agent definition had ZERO guidance on this field, so it improvised a spec-contradicting high-severity BLOCK -- and demanding removal of a schema-required field (>=2 ordinals) made it unfixable, burning the DA-retry budget (same failure class as B0043). Two gaps: (1) the DA over-flagged legitimate subsets; (2) no deterministic guard existed for the genuine leak (an ordinal NOT in this regime's episode set).
value_rationale: High: episode-targeting is the exact lookahead vector the Phase 5 firewall exists to prevent, and it currently slips past the automated gate. A lint rule makes the contract deterministic and frees the DA to focus on economic substance.
recommendation: Done (Option A, refined, per expert adjudication): (1) Added lint_anti_circularity rule anti_circularity_episode_ordinals_not_subset -- FAILs only when target_regime_episode_ordinals is NOT a subset of dossier.regime_episode_ordinals. Optional dossier param (skip-on-None for back-compat); threaded via the _load_dossier already in stage_review for B0043. format_lint_feedback gained a subset-constraint line. (2) Calibrated .claude/agents/phase5-devils-advocate.md with an episode-ordinal bullet per SKILL.md:137 (subset legitimate, do not demand removal, global-timeline ordinals fine, single-episode-collapse is a CAVEAT not a BLOCK). Tests: tests/phase5/test_b0044_episode_ordinal_subset_lint.py (5 cases incl. tick-13 subset must PASS). Full suite 587 passed. Note: T013/T013D1/T013D2 ordinal objections were spec-contradicting DA misfires; do NOT resurrect the inverted premise.
out_of_scope: Not redesigning the dossier; not touching B0043's dossier-threading fix.

history.reason: fix(B0043): thread regime_stats_dossier into the devil's-advocate payload
Re-scoped after methodology review: SKILL.md:137 says a subset of regime_episode_ordinals is the LEGITIMATE shape-leakage tripwire; only a NON-subset is a violation.
history.from: Anti-circularity lint should flag named episode ordinals in lookahead_shape_attestation
history.to: Episode-ordinal subset lint + DA calibration (B0044 premise INVERTED: subsets are legitimate)

history.from: lookahead_shape_attestation.target_regime_episode_ordinals lets the hypothesizer enumerate WHICH historical regime episodes the hypothesis is aimed at. Naming a hand-picked subset is a backdoor for selecting on known historical price action even when the causal story reads clean. In tick 13 the DA raised this as high-severity on two of three revisions, but lint_anti_circularity has no rule for it, so the proposals passed lint and only the (now dossier-blind, see B0043) DA caught it. The firewall should be enforced mechanically, not left to the DA.
history.to: Loop A tick 13's BULL_STRESSED proposals were BLOCKED partly because the DA read target_regime_episode_ordinals naming a SUBSET ([13,15,21], [1,13,21]) as episode-targeting. Per phase5-regime-methodology SKILL.md:137 a subset is the INTENDED design and only a non-subset should be flagged; the DA agent definition had ZERO guidance on this field, so it improvised a spec-contradicting high-severity BLOCK -- and demanding removal of a schema-required field (>=2 ordinals) made it unfixable, burning the DA-retry budget (same failure class as B0043). Two gaps: (1) the DA over-flagged legitimate subsets; (2) no deterministic guard existed for the genuine leak (an ordinal NOT in this regime's episode set).

history.from: Add a lint_anti_circularity rule: target_regime_episode_ordinals must equal the dossier's FULL regime_episode_ordinals set (the mechanical falsification scope), OR be replaced by a count-only field (n_episodes) that carries no episode identity. FAIL if it names a strict subset. Decide with the methodology spec whether to keep the ordinals field at all vs. collapse it to a count. Update phase5-regime-methodology SKILL + proposal schema docs to match.
history.to: Done (Option A, refined, per expert adjudication): (1) Added lint_anti_circularity rule anti_circularity_episode_ordinals_not_subset -- FAILs only when target_regime_episode_ordinals is NOT a subset of dossier.regime_episode_ordinals. Optional dossier param (skip-on-None for back-compat); threaded via the _load_dossier already in stage_review for B0043. format_lint_feedback gained a subset-constraint line. (2) Calibrated .claude/agents/phase5-devils-advocate.md with an episode-ordinal bullet per SKILL.md:137 (subset legitimate, do not demand removal, global-timeline ordinals fine, single-episode-collapse is a CAVEAT not a BLOCK). Tests: tests/phase5/test_b0044_episode_ordinal_subset_lint.py (5 cases incl. tick-13 subset must PASS). Full suite 587 passed. Note: T013/T013D1/T013D2 ordinal objections were spec-contradicting DA misfires; do NOT resurrect the inverted premise.

history.reason: Shipped lint subset-guard + DA calibration (premise inverted from the original draft). 587 tests pass.
